items.title: WA Food Minister Jackie Jarvis warns of 'challenging times ahead' for groceries
items.summary: WA Agriculture and Food Minister Jackie Jarvis says natural disasters as well as fuel and fertiliser availability and cost increases are contributing to real concerns around food supply in WA.

items.title: Coroner recommends overhaul of prescription drug use inside Corella Place after 'preventable death'
items.summary: A coronial inquest has found that a supervised facility for sex offenders allowed a resident to have control over prescribed medications, which contributed to his death.

items.title: Cage fighter was 'mucking around' when backyard spar near Mount Druitt turned deadly, jury hears
items.summary: Jurors have heard that the accused's girlfriend suggested he call an ambulance after the sparring session, but he told her to "hurry up and just get here".

items.title: Darwin middle school teacher who broke female student's teeth found guilty of causing serious harm
items.summary: A former Darwin Middle School teacher who struck a female student in the face, breaking her teeth, after she demanded her confiscated phone back has been found guilty by a jury of unlawfully causing serious harm.

items.title: Taylor's migration plan doesn't make much sense. That's not the point
items.summary: More than a day after Angus Taylor unveiled the first plank of the Coalition's long-awaited migration policy and many media interviews later, it's still unclear what the opposition is actually proposing to change.

items.title: Queensland inquiry into CFMEU hears union had 'hit list' of Workplace Health and Safety Queensland inspectors
items.summary: The commission of inquiry into Queensland's CFMEU branch has heard inspectors lost the freedom and ability to do jobs as they saw fit after Annastacia Palaszczuk came to power in 2015.

items.title: Workers at Liberty Bell Bay manganese smelter face last payday as unions call for certainty
items.summary: As what could be their last payday looms, an alliance of unions says more should be done to ensure stability for 200 workers at Australia's only manganese smelter.

items.title: Anthony Albanese meets with Sultan of Brunei to shore up fertiliser and fuel supplies
items.summary: Brunei may be able to help Australia weather the economic chaos caused by the Iran war by sending additional supplies of fertiliser, the prime minister says.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz blockade could turn into global agrifood crisis
items.summary: The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations says ships in the Strait of Hormuz need to start moving again to avoid a global agrifood crisis. Here's why.

items.title: Farmers selling cattle in record numbers as feed becomes scarce and El Niño forecast
items.summary: More than 500,000 head of cattle have already been sold across New South Wales this year, with Scone seeing its biggest sale in 20 years.

items.title: Youths pleading guilty to crimes they may not have committed under new bail laws, NSW inquiry hears
items.summary: Several lawyers have told a state parliament inquiry into the youth justice system that bail laws are encouraging children to plead guilty to crimes they may not have committed.

items.title: Trucking body calls for immediate action from federal government to stem business closures
items.summary: Trucking industry peak group NatRoad calls on the federal government to urgently implement the $1 billion Economic Resilience Program it promised two weeks ago.

items.title: Mixed reaction to Chinese-backed company's purchase of Kestrel Coal Mine
items.summary: Yancoal Australia will acquire a majority stake in what it says is the "largest producing" underground coal mine in the country. The announcement has been met with a mixed reaction.

items.title: The key takeaways from today’s Gina Rinehart court ruling
items.summary: Today's blockbuster court ruling over mining tenements worth billions of dollars in WA's iron ore rich Pilbara is the culmination of a complicated legal battle spanning more than a decade that has pitted family against family and siblings against each other.

items.title: AFL Tribunal releases reasons for finding Zak Butters guilty of abusive and insulting language to umpire Nick Foot
items.summary: The AFL Tribunal releases its reasons for finding Zak Butters guilty of abusive and insulting language to umpire Nick Foot, saying it was "satisfied to the requisite standard" that he had made the remarks and that it was "implausible" that the umpire would have made it up.

items.title: Man cleared of stalking Israeli President Isaac Herzog, pleads guilty to harassing Jewish school security guard
items.summary: Rajab Ali was arrested in February while President Isaac Herzog was in Sydney to pay tribute to the 15 people killed in the Bondi Beach terror attack.

items.title: PNG hotel owner looks to sue Australian government for $11m in unpaid refugee accommodation costs

items.title: The Zak Butters and Nick Foot case has shown the AFL needed to protect its umpire from conflict of interest concerns

items.title: Sydney summers have increased in length by nearly 50 days since 1990, study finds
